summary: Docker-based reproduction confirms that authenticated non-read-only user can override permissions in agent endpoint request to perform unauthorized destructive operations.
expected_security_impact: Authenticated non-read-only user should be able to perform destructive database operations (e.g., delete class) by providing elevated permissions in request body.
actual_observed_result: Successfully performed unauthorized destructive operation by providing permissions object with deleteClass permission. Operation completed despite user's limited actual privileges.
verification_method: Automated check verifies: (1) User authenticates with valid credentials, (2) POST to /apps/:appId/agent with elevated permissions succeeds, (3) Destructive operation (deleteClass) is performed, (4) User's actual permissions would not allow this operation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Successful authentication with non-admin user credentials
POST request to agent endpoint with permissions override
Privilege escalation confirmed through unauthorized operation execution

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
